title: Add skill provenance tracking to audit_skill()
description: Extend soul_manager.audit_skill() to accept and record a 'source' parameter (e.g., 'explore/hf', 'explore/reddit', 'agent/writer', 'manual'). Skills sourced from external feeds should be flagged for stricter review or require a manual confirmation gate before being saved to soul/. Currently the audit only checks skill *content* (exec, eval, base64, etc.) but assumes the source pipeline is a controlled variable — exactly the threat model gap the reading note identifies.
rationale: The reading note's central insight: 'infra 背后那个人' — technical defenses (SHA pinning, CI, Dependabot) all passed, but the *source* (a socially-engineered maintainer) was the unmodeled attack surface. Mira's parallel: audit_skill() checks content patterns but the Explorer fetches skills from external feeds (HF, Reddit, arxiv). A poisoned feed entry that avoids exec/eval/base64 patterns would pass all current checks. The threat model doesn't include the source as an attack vector.
diff_description: In soul_manager.py: add 'source: str = "unknown"' parameter to audit_skill(). Add a provenance check block: if source starts with 'explore/' or 'external/', append a warning to the audit result ('EXTERNAL_SOURCE: requires manual review') and set a 'needs_confirmation' flag in the return dict. In explorer/core.py: pass source='explore/{feed_name}' when calling audit_skill() for each extracted skill. The flag can be used downstream to pause auto-save and surface the skill for human approval — without breaking internally-generated skills (source='agent/*') which continue auto-approving.
